data.title: ASX Midday Sector Update: Healthcare Sector Jumps, Information Technology Stocks Struggle
data.description:
The healthcare sector climbed 1.11% at midday Tuesday.

Stocks rallied after some volatility on Wall Street, with investors cautiously optimistic after a temporary pause on tariffs on some electronic devices. The S&P 500 closed up 2.2%.

Botanix Pharmaceuticals received firm commitments to raise AU$40 million via a non-underwritten institutional placement, its shares rose 9% in midday trade.

On the flip side, the information technology stocks fell, shedding 0.45%.

EROAD does not expect any material impact of the US' 10% blanket tariffs on its revenue or earnings, generating 44% of its revenue from the US market, with roughly 88% being from services not subject to the US import tariffs. It produces most of its hardware products through manufacturers in Indonesia, the Philippines, and Vietnam.

data.title: Wereldhave Buys Three Adjacent Retail Units in Shopping Complex in Netherlands
data.description:
Wereldhave WHA.AS) said Monday it bought three adjacent retail units in the Pieter Vreedeplein shopping complex in Tilburg, The Netherlands, from an unnamed single-family office.

The Dutch real estate investment company paid 5.4 million euros for the properties, with settlement carried out through the issuance of 313,700 new shares at market value.

The units have a total gross leasable area of 2,756 square meters and are leased to mixed-use tenants. Wereldhave said the transaction was marginally accretive to its EPS.

data.title: Sika Logs Higher Q1 Net Sales; Affirms FY25 Outlook
data.description:
Sika said Tuesday that its net sales rose year over year in the first quarter of 2025.

The Swiss specialty chemicals group's net sales were 2.68 billion francs, compared with 2.65 billion francs a year ago.

Sika affirmed its outlook of a 3% to 6% growth in sales in local currencies for full-year 2025, while also reiterating its medium-term targets for sustainable, profitable growth in 2028.

data.title: Market Chatter: Japan to Boost Economic Security with Undersea Cables, Satellites, Rockets
data.description:
Japan plans to classify undersea cables, satellites, and rockets as critical goods for economic security, among other items vulnerable to overseas supply shocks, Nikkei Asia reported on Tuesday.

The Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry will propose the additions at an expert meeting starting Tuesday, with revisions to the economic security plan expected as early as May, Nikkei said.

The move comes as 99% of Japan's international communications rely on undersea cables, the report added.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Hengrui Medicine Gets Nod to Trial Inflammation Treatment Drug
data.description:
Jiangsu Hengrui Medicine's unit, Guangdong Hengrui Pharmaceuticals, will conduct clinical trials on SHR-1819 injection after obtaining approval from China's National Medical Products Administration, according to a Shanghai Stock Exchange filing on Monday.

The drug will be tested as a treatment for type 2 inflammation-related diseases.

The investment into the research and development of the drug reached 149.7 million yuan, the pharmaceutical company said.

The company's shares fell less than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Insig AI Secures Contract with UK Financial Regulator
data.description:
Insig AI signed a subscription service license agreement with the UK Financial Conduct Authority for its Transparency and Disclosure Index.

The order also includes access to ImpactScope's Greenwashing Identifier, the data science and machine learning services company said Monday. Insig AI holds the exclusive rights to sell ImpactScope's products to international regulators and asset managers.

data.title: On-Chain Clues Suggest Bitcoin Bounce Might Be a False Signal—Here’s What to Know
data.description:
Bitcoin has shown signs of stabilization following its earlier correction this month, which saw the asset fall to as low as $74,000. Over the past week, Bitcoin has rebounded strongly, gaining nearly 10%, and now trades above $84,000.

This upward movement has reignited optimism among investors, though some analysts remain cautious about calling this a definitive trend reversal.

Apparent Demand Shows Recovery, But Trend Reversal Uncertain

According to recent on-chain data, the current recovery in BTC may be linked to improving demand indicators. However, it is suggested that the broader market structure still needs to confirm whether this bounce reflects a sustainable rally or is merely a temporary pause in the ongoing correction.

CryptoQuant contributor Kripto Mevsimi particularly drew attention to Bitcoin’s Apparent Demand metric, specifically the 30-day sum, which has started to rebound from negative territory.

This trend is being observed as a potential sign of changing market dynamics. However, Mevsimi warns against assuming this is the start of a new bullish cycle, drawing parallels to Bitcoin’s behavior during the latter part of the 2021 cycle.

During that period, demand remained suppressed for an extended timeframe, even as prices temporarily recovered. Only after a long consolidation phase did the market experience a genuine structural shift.

Mevsimi highlights that although momentum may be improving, more time and confirmation are necessary before a macro-level trend reversal can be confirmed.

Bitcoin Short-Term Holder Selling Pressure Declines on Binance

Another market signal worth watching comes from Binance, one of the largest crypto exchanges by trading volume. CryptoQuant analyst Darkfost reports that inflows of Bitcoin from short-term holders (STHs) to Binance have been steadily decreasing, suggesting a decline in immediate selling pressure.

The data indicates that average realized prices for STHs currently hover around $92,800, meaning many recent sellers have exited at a loss.

Darkfost notes that inflows from STHs dropped from approximately 17,000 BTC in November to around 9,000 BTC more recently. This downtrend in selling could provide some support for Bitcoin’s current price levels.

Still, the analyst emphasizes the need for continued monitoring to determine if this reduction in selling pressure persists. The easing of short-term holder activity could reduce overhead resistance and contribute to market stability, but confirmation of accumulation or a broader bullish phase remains elusive.

Darkfost
@Darkfost_Coc

STH selling pressure declining on Binance

Tracking $BTC inflows on Binance is a useful way to visualize potential selling pressure, as the platform handles significant trading volumes.

Short Term Holders have been under considerable stress recently, with many even ending up… pic.twitter.com/lwOe45H7L3

data.title: China XD Electric's 2024 Profit Jumps 18%
data.description:
China XD Electric's attributable profit rose 18% to 1.05 billion yuan in 2024 from 897.2 million yuan in 2023, after adjustments, according to an annual report filed Tuesday with the Shanghai bourse.

Earnings per share at the electrical equipment manufacturer increased 18% to 0.2057 yuan from 0.1750 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 5.6% to 22.2 billion yuan from 21 billion yuan a year earlier.

China XD's shares slipped 1% during Tuesday morning trading.

data.title: Iron ore consolidates as market awaits more China data
data.description:
Iron ore futures prices dangled in a tight range on Tuesday, as investors and traders were awaiting a raft of more economic data from top consumer China for clarity of demand outlook and stimulus prospects.

The most-traded September iron ore contract on China's Dalian Commodity Exchange (DCE) was 0.85% higher at 712 yuan ($97.41) a metric ton, as of 0316 GMT.

The benchmark May iron ore (SZZFK5) on the Singapore Exchange was, however, 0.29% lower at $97.85 a ton as of 0310 GMT.

China is scheduled to release a batch of more economic indicators and industrial metals output data on Wednesday.

Mixed market signals have blurred demand outlook for the key steelmaking ingredient, leaving prices swinging back and forth in a narrow range.

Relatively high hot metal output underpinned near-term ore demand, arresting a potential price slump amid the escalating trade tensions between the world's two largest economies.

But it's hard to see hot metal output surpass 2.45 million tons without substantial good news for the steel sector, analysts at Maike Futures said in a note.

Moreover, while China's steel exports in March beat expectations to exceed 10 million tons, outbound shipments in the second half of the year will likely feel the pain from the intensifying trade frictions fueled by the heightened tariffs by U.S. President Donald Trump, said analysts.

UBS has downgraded China's GDP growth forecast to 3.4% in 2025. A potentially slower economic growth indicates weaker demand for industrial metals products.

Other steelmaking ingredients on the DCE advanced, with coking coal NYMEX:ACT1! and coke (DCJcv1) up 0.17% and 0.72%, respectively.

Most steel benchmarks on the Shanghai Futures Exchange retreated. Rebar dipped 0.26%, hot-rolled coil shed 0.28%, wire rod (SWRcv1) languished 0.24% while stainless steel nudged up 0.12%.

($1 = 7.3091 Chinese yuan renminbi)

data.title: Agility Global's Unit to Buy US Aviation Support Services Provider G2 Secure Staff
data.description:
Agility Global's (ADX:AGILITY) subsidiary Menzies Aviation agreed to buy US-based aviation support services provider G2 Secure Staff, according to a Monday release.

Menzies Aviation will purchase the business at an enterprise value of $305 million and a deferred consideration of $10 million, payable in 2026, subject to achieving certain performance-based targets.

The transaction will be financed by current external credit facilities and is anticipated to be finalized in June, pending regulatory approvals.

data.title: Star Entertainment Group Swings to Fiscal H1 Loss as Revenue Declines
data.description:
Star Entertainment Group reported Tuesday a loss per diluted share of AU$0.105 in the fiscal first half, compared with earnings of AU$0.004 per diluted share a year earlier.

Analysts polled by Visible Alpha were expecting a loss of AU$0.04.

Revenue for the six months ended Dec. 31, 2024, was AU$724 million, down from AU$865.7 million in the same period a year earlier. Analysts surveyed by Visible Alpha expected AU$664.7 million.

The company said that material uncertainty remains over its ability to continue as a going concern, with its short-term liquidity outlook hinging on several key initiatives including the completion of the AU$300 million investment by Bally's and Investment Holdings, the release of proceeds from the sale of the Event Centre, and the finalisation of its exit from the Destination Brisbane Consortium joint venture.

data.title: Indonesia's Consumer Confidence Drops in March
data.description:
Indonesia's Consumer Confidence Index came in at 121.1 in March, down from 126.4 in February but still in positive territory, according to a Tuesday release from Bank Indonesia, citing its latest Consumer Survey.

The central bank attributed the sustained positive sentiment to a strong Current Economic Condition Index (CECI) and Consumer Expectation Index (CEI).

The CECI remained in optimistic territory at 110.6, down from 114.2 the month earlier, while CEI came in at 131.7, retreating from 138.7 a month earlier.

data.title: US 10-Year Treasury Yield Declines for Second Session
data.description:
The yield on the 10-year US Treasury note fell to around 4.35% on Tuesday, marking its second consecutive day decline as investor sentiment surrounding the US economic outlook began to stabilize.

The easing in yields followed President Donald Trump's decision to exempt key technology products from new reciprocal tariffs, along with reports suggesting a possible pause on the 25% levies targeting auto imports.

However, market uncertainty persisted after the US Commerce Department launched a national security investigation into semiconductor and pharmaceutical imports—both critical sectors in global supply chains.

Meanwhile, Fed Governor Christopher Waller signaled a dovish tilt in monetary policy, stating that inflation driven by tariffs is likely to be transitory.

He also emphasized that the Fed remains prepared to cut interest rates further if necessary, reaffirming the central bank’s commitment to supporting growth over combating short-term, trade-induced inflation pressures.

data.title: Australia's Brazilian Rare Earths extends gains eyeing strongest session ever
data.description:
** Shares of Brazilian Rare Earths extend gains to rise as much as 24.4% to A$2.4, their highest since January 22

** Stock last up 20.2% at A$1.93, set for strongest session ever

** The rare earths miner on Monday amended acquisition terms with Rio Tinto Brazil to replace milestone payment with production-based royalty payment for the Amargosa Bauxite project in Brazil

** About 262,613 shares change hands, 1.1x the 30-day average of 232,298

** Stock down 1.7% YTD, including moves in current session

data.title: Korea Electric Power Signs Deal with Vietnam's State Utility on Power Grid, New Energy Cooperation
data.description:
Korea Electric Power signed a memorandum of understanding with Vietnam's state-run power transmission company to collaborate on power grid development and new energy industries.

The agreement was part of a broader scenario to boost bilateral energy ties during ministerial meetings held in Hanoi, the Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy said Tuesday.

Shares of Korea Electric Power rose more than 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Duality Bio Shares Surge 91% in Hong Kong Debut, Defying Market Turmoil
data.description:
Duality Biotherapeutics made a stellar trading debut in Hong Kong on Tuesday morning as its shares soared over 91% at the opening bell, defying the stock market volatility caused by the tariff war between the world's two largest economies.

The Chinese biotech firm opened at HK$181 per share, well above its initial public offering price of HK$94.60. As of 10:41 a.m., Hong Kong time, the stock traded at HK$209.60.

The company's debut takes place against a backdrop of heightened uncertainty in global equity markets, driven by escalating US-China trade tensions.

data.title: Australia's 'Big Four' Banks Expected to Weather Weaker Economic Conditions Over 2025, Fitch Ratings Says
data.description:
Fitch Ratings expects the "Big Four" Australian banks to withstand an expected low-growth and rising unemployment environment over the current year due to significant buffers, the credit ratings agency said in a statement Tuesday.

The strong business profiles and solid financial metrics of National Australia Bank , ANZ Group Holdings , Westpac Banking , and Commonwealth Bank of Australia underpin their long-term issuer default rating of "AA-."

The outlook on Commonwealth Bank of Australia's rating was revised to positive, while the other three banks retain their stable outlooks. The revision reflects the outperformance of the lender's earning metrics. It also revised the funding and liquidity score of the four banks to "a+" from "a."

Collateral positions are strong across the banks on the back of strengthened mortgage underwriting. The lenders' asset quality is expected to weaken modestly, but impaired loans are forecast to peak this year. Their strong liquidity management continues to offset some of the risks from their reliance on offshore wholesale funding sources.

The Australian government is likely to support these banks in times of financial strain due to their systemic importance, Fitch said.

data.title: Valneva's Chikungunya Vaccine Gets Brazil Approval
data.description:
Valneva received market authorization from the Brazilian Health Regulatory Agency for its chikungunya vaccine, IXCHIQ.

The French specialty vaccine company said Monday that the approval covers the single-use vaccine for people aged at least 18 years in the endemic country. The approval enables the start of large-scale clinical trials in Brazil, including phase 4 trials supporting the vaccine's approval in the US and the European Union.

IXCHIQ is already approved for specific age groups in the EU, US, Canada, and the UK, with Valneva having applications pending to extend its use to adolescents in the US, Canada, and the UK.

data.title: Inner Mongolia ERDOS Resources 2024 Profit Falls 36%, Revenue Slips 7%
data.description:
Inner Mongolia ERDOS Resources posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 1.85 billion yuan, down 36% from 2.90 billion yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share slid to 0.66 yuan from 1.04 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The cashmere apparel and metal producer's revenue declined 7.0% year over year to 28.4 billion yuan from 30.6 billion yuan.

Inner Mongolia ERDOS Resources declared a dividend of 0.60 per share.

Shares of the company were down 2% in recent trade.

data.title: Wuhan Dameng Database's 2024 Profit Jumps 22%; Shares Slide 7%
data.description:
Wuhan Dameng Database's attributable profit rose 22% to 361.9 million yuan in 2024 from 296.1 million yuan in 2023, according to an annual report filed Tuesday with the Shanghai bourse.

Earnings per share at the database management system design and development company increased 4.8% to 5.44 yuan from 5.19 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 31% to 1.04 billion yuan from 794.3 million yuan a year earlier.

Dameng Database's shares slid nearly 7% during Tuesday's late-morning trading.

data.title: Zhejiang Longsheng Group Logs 32% Rise in 2024 Profit; Shares Up 6%
data.description:
Zhejiang Longsheng Group posted 2024 net profit attributable to shareholders of 2.03 billion yuan, up 32% from 1.53 billion yuan the previous year.

Earnings per share rose to 0.6320 yuan from 0.4861 yuan, according to a filing with the Shanghai bourse.

The chemical manufacturer's revenue rose 3.8% year over year to 15.9 billion yuan from 15.3 billion yuan.

Zhejiang Longsheng Group declared a dividend of 0.25 per share.

Shares of the company were up 6% in recent trade.

data.title: South Korea, Vietnam Pledge Deeper Trade Ties and Energy Cooperation
data.description:
South Korea and Vietnam agreed to boost bilateral trade to $150 billion by 2030 and expand collaboration in nuclear power, liquefied natural gas, clean energy, and textiles during ministerial meetings in Hanoi.

The two sides also discussed free trade agreement amendments to ease customs, expand agri-trade, and enhance certification processes, the South Korean Ministry of Trade, Industry and Energy said in a Tuesday release.

data.title: Italian Court Validates Class Action Against Stellantis Over Japanese-made Faulty Airbags
data.description:
An Italian civil court verified the validity of the class action against Stellantis (STLAM.MI, ) over Japanese-made faulty airbags in its vehicles, Reuters reported Monday, citing the European carmaker and two consumer groups behind the legal action.

Separate statements from consumer groups Codacons and Altroconsumo said the court hearing was adjourned to Nov. 21 after the court in Turin, Italy, ruled that the case was admissible. They said affected customers have 150 days to join the class action.

The European carmaker sourced the airbags under scrutiny in the case from Japanese automotive parts manufacturer Takata. In addition to the pending case in Italy, it is also facing a similar class action in France.

Stellantis told Reuters that the latest ruling only addressed the admissibility of the case, not its alleged liability or the damages claimed. It noted that it was working "tirelessly" on recalling affected models and reserving "the right to consider any appropriate initiative in the context of the ongoing proceedings."

Stellantis did not immediately respond to a request for comment from MT Newswires.

data.title: Duality Biotherapeutics Raises HK$1.5 Billion From BioNTech-backed Hong Kong IPO
data.description:
Duality Biotherapeutics raised HK$1.51 billion in net proceeds from its IPO after pricing the shares at the lower end of the indicative price range.

The developer of antibody drug conjugates issued 17,332,300 shares at HK$94.60, the bottom end of its guidance of as high as HK$103.20 each, according to a late Monday disclosure.

The deal's Hong Kong public offering portion was 115.14 times subscribed, prompting the company to issue 7.5 million shares, a fivefold increase from the 1.5 million shares initially allotted.

The international offering portion was 13.52 times subscribed, and the firm issued 9.79 million shares, lower than the initial allocation of 13.56 million.

Germany's BioNTech and 10 private equity funds were cornerstone investors in the listing. They subscribed to a total of 5.34 million IPO shares.

Duality Bio starts trading on the Hong Kong bourse today, April 15.

data.title: Australia's Clarity Pharmaceutical gains on reporting first prostate cancer treatment
data.description:
** Shares of Clarity Pharmaceuticals rise 6% to A$1.850,

** Stock hits its highest level since April 3

** Radiopharmaceutical co announces the treatment of the first participant with the first dose of Cu-SAR-bisPSMA, a radioactive substance used to target prostate cancer cells

** CU6 down 55.7% YTD, including current session's moves

data.title: This iron ore miner is no Fortescue – But it plans to triple production in 2025
data.description:
Key points:
  • Fenix Resources plans to nearly triple iron ore production from 1.5Mtpa to 4Mtpa in 2024-25, with Shine mine at full capacity and Beebyn-W11 nearing production
  • Record shipments of 704,000 tonnes in March 2025 quarter, almost double last year’s, signal strong operational momentum
  • Bell Potter is Buy rated with a 41-cent target price (as at Jan-25) reflects confidence in Fenix’s low-capital growth and integrated logistics driving robust cash flows
  • Dividend pause in August 2024 to fund expansion caused an 11.9% share price drop, but analysts expect dividends to resume in 2025 with 5-6% yields by 2026-27

While iron ore remains a cornerstone of Australia’s export economy, the pool of pure-play investment opportunities is shrinking. Heavyweights like BHP and Rio Tinto are steadily shifting their focus to future-facing metals like copper and lithium. Among larger-cap iron ore companies, Fortescue — gradually diversifying into green energy — and Champion Iron stand out.

In terms of larger cap pure plays, this leaves us with Fortescue – which will eventually become a more diversified green energy producer – and Champion Iron.

However, at the smaller end of the market, Fenix Resources is arguably one of the only iron ore names poised for major growth this year.

Fenix at a Glance

Based in Western Australia’s Mid-West region, Fenix operates across three integrated divisions:

  • Iron Ore: Mining and exploration through its fully owned Iron Ridge mine (high-grade, direct shipping ore), Shine mine (open-pit, DSO), and W11 deposit (rights to mine up to 10Mt).

  • Logistics: Road and rail haulage solutions for Fenix’s operations and third-party clients.

  • Port Services: In-loading via truck or rail with secure storage for up to 400,000 tonnes at Fenix’s on-wharf facilities.

In FY24, Fenix generated $259.2 million in revenue, with $240.1 million driven by iron ore sales.

Scaling Up: From 1.5Mtpa to 4Mtpa

The 2024-25 period marks a pivotal shift for Fenix as it boosts iron ore production from 1.5 million tonnes to 4 million tonnes per annum. The company’s latest quarterly report (15 April 2025) highlighted recent developments: "Shine is now in full production, Iron Ridge continues to perform, and our third mine, Beebyn-W11, is moving rapidly towards first production."

The March 2025 quarter saw record shipments of 704,000 tonnes — nearly double the previous year’s figure — fueled by Shine’s ramp-up. Fenix also trimmed cash costs at Iron Ridge by 4.8%, dropping to A$73.8 per tonne from A$77.6 a year earlier, signaling operational efficiency.

Analyst Outlook

Bell Potter analysts reaffirmed a Buy rating in January 2025, with a 41-cent target price. Their optimism rests on Fenix’s ability to “expand its portfolio of low-capital mining assets and leverage integrated logistics to drive strong cash flows.” These cash flows are expected to fund growth initiatives and shareholder returns. Analysts project a significant earnings increase from FY24 to FY26, alongside a return to dividends:

2024a

Source: Bell Potter | January 2025

Recent performance

Fenix shares have remained flat year-to-date and over the past 12 months. A notable setback came in August 2024 when the company announced it would pause dividends to fund current growth plans.

The market did not take the news well, with Fenix shares down 11.9% on the day of the announcement (29-Aug-24). Compounding the pressure, iron ore prices slipped from ~US$100 to US$92 per tonne that month.

Historically, Fenix has been a reliable high-yield stock, delivering 10-15% annual dividend yields. The shift toward growth may have pushed out some income-focused investors, but the company’s progress on its expansion plans — and the anticipated resumption of dividends — could serve as a key catalyst for share price recovery.

data.title: Bank of Beijing's 2024 Profit Inches Up
data.description:
Bank of Beijing's attributable profit inched up 0.8% to 25.8 billion yuan in 2024 from 25.6 billion yuan in 2023, according to an annual report filed Tuesday with the Shanghai bourse.

The lender's earnings per share increased 0.9% to 1.07 yuan from 1.06 yuan in the previous year.

Operating income grew 4.8% to 69.9 billion yuan from 66.7 billion yuan a year earlier.
